name: IMF Articles of Agreement 1944 (International Monetary Fund)
summary: The Articles of Agreement of the International Monetary Fund (IMF Articles of Agreement) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-IMF-treaty framework adopted by the 44 founding states at Bretton Woods — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-monetary-cooperation framework + foundational Bretton Woods framework alongside World Bank framework (IMF Articles of Agreement adopted 22 July 1944 at Bretton Woods Conference in Bretton Woods New Hampshire USA by 44 founding states + effective 27 December 1945 + IMF operations began 1 March 1947 — formal foundational IMF treaty framework establishing comprehensive IMF organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-monetary-cooperation framework + foundational Bretton Woods framework alongside World Bank framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal weighted-voting framework via quota-based-voting framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal SDR Special Drawing Rights framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IMF Conditionality framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Soviet-non-participation framework via Soviet Union signed but never ratified IMF Articles of Agreement framework + Cold War-era unique framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal IMF Special Drawing Rights SDR framework (IMF Special Drawing Rights SDR established 1969 via First Amendment to IMF Articles of Agreement effective 28 July 1969 providing formal supplementary international reserve asset framework — formal foundational IMF SDR framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal supplementary international reserve asset framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal currency basket framework with USD + EUR + JPY + GBP + CNY effective 1 October 2016 framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal IMF Conditionality framework (IMF Conditionality framework providing formal conditional-lending framework + Structural Adjustment Programmes SAPs framework + Stand-By Arrangements SBA framework + Extended Fund Facility EFF framework + Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust PRGT framework + Resilience and Sustainability Trust RST framework effective 2022 — formal foundational IMF Conditionality framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Conditionality framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal IMF Quota Reform framework (IMF Quota Reform framework providing formal voting-power redistribution framework via 14th General Review of Quotas effective 26 January 2016 + 15th General Review concluded 13 February 2020 + 16th General Review concluded 15 December 2023 — formal foundational IMF Quota Reform framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal voting-power redistribution framework with operational Quota Reform framework including BRICS Plus emerging-economies share increase framework). The IMF Articles of Agreement is binding upon 191 IMF member states as of 2026 — substantial universal coverage framework with Andorra + Cuba + North Korea + Liechtenstein + Monaco + Nauru + Vatican non-members framework. The Articles are silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal IMF Conditionality framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal IMF-Conditionality-on-Social-Spending framework affecting family-policy-spending framework. The IMF Board of Governors + IMF Executive Board + IMF Managing Director + IMF Staff are the formal IMF bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The IMF Articles of Agreement is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: IMF Articles of Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF Articles of Agreement adopted 22 July 1944 at Bretton Woods Conference in Bretton Woods New Hampshire USA + effective 27 December 1945 — formal foundational IMF treaty framework establishing comprehensive IMF organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: IMF First Amendment
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF First Amendment to Articles of Agreement effective 28 July 1969 establishing Special Drawing Rights SDR — formal foundational IMF SDR framework.

statutory_framework.citation: IMF Second Amendment 1978 (Floating Exchange Rate Framework)
statutory_framework.title: IMF Second Amendment
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF Second Amendment to Articles of Agreement effective 1 April 1978 legalising floating exchange rate framework following Bretton Woods system collapse 1971 — formal foundational IMF post-Bretton-Woods framework.

statutory_framework.title: IMF 14th General Review
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF 14th General Review of Quotas effective 26 January 2016 doubling IMF quotas + redistributing voting power toward emerging economies — formal IMF Quota Reform framework.

statutory_framework.citation: IMF SDR Currency Basket Inclusion of CNY Renminbi 2016
statutory_framework.title: IMF SDR Renminbi Inclusion
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF SDR Currency Basket Inclusion of CNY Renminbi effective 1 October 2016 — formal IMF SDR Currency Basket framework including USD + EUR + JPY + GBP + CNY.

statutory_framework.citation: IMF Resilience and Sustainability Trust RST 2022
statutory_framework.title: IMF RST
statutory_framework.relevance: IMF Resilience and Sustainability Trust RST effective 1 May 2022 — formal IMF long-term-climate-and-pandemic-resilience financing framework.

apex_courts.name: IMF Board of Governors
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for IMF.

apex_courts.name: IMF Managing Director
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for IMF — traditionally European Managing Director framework.

apex_courts.name: IMF International Monetary and Financial Committee (IMFC)

professional_regulators.name: IMF Staff
professional_regulators.role: IMF Articles of Agreement implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: IMF-related communications operate via state-state framework via IMF Board of Governors + IMF Executive Board; individual communications not formally accepted under IMF framework.

key_developments.title: Bretton Woods Conference + IMF Articles of Agreement
key_developments.description: Bretton Woods Conference held 1-22 July 1944 in Bretton Woods New Hampshire USA + IMF Articles of Agreement adopted 22 July 1944 + World Bank Articles of Agreement adopted 22 July 1944 — formal foundational IMF + Bretton Woods system framework adopted.

key_developments.title: IMF Articles of Agreement enter force
key_developments.description: IMF Articles of Agreement entered force 27 December 1945 — formal foundational IMF treaty framework operational.

key_developments.title: IMF operations begin
key_developments.description: IMF operations began 1 March 1947 — formal foundational IMF operational framework operational.

key_developments.title: IMF SDR established
key_developments.description: IMF Special Drawing Rights SDR established 1969 via First Amendment to IMF Articles of Agreement effective 28 July 1969 — formal foundational IMF SDR framework operational.

key_developments.title: Bretton Woods system collapse
key_developments.description: Bretton Woods system collapsed 15 August 1971 following Nixon Shock terminating US Dollar convertibility to gold — formal Bretton Woods system collapse framework.

key_developments.title: IMF Second Amendment legalising floating exchange rates
key_developments.description: IMF Second Amendment to Articles of Agreement effective 1 April 1978 legalising floating exchange rate framework — formal foundational IMF post-Bretton-Woods framework operational.

key_developments.title: Global Financial Crisis + IMF role expansion
key_developments.description: Global Financial Crisis 2008-2009 + IMF role expansion via G20 + IMF SDR allocation USD 250 billion equivalent + IMF lending expansion framework — formal IMF Global-Financial-Crisis response framework.

key_developments.title: IMF 14th General Review of Quotas + SDR CNY Inclusion
key_developments.description: IMF 14th General Review of Quotas effective 26 January 2016 + SDR Currency Basket Inclusion of CNY Renminbi effective 1 October 2016 — formal IMF Quota Reform + SDR Currency Basket framework operational.

key_developments.title: IMF historic SDR allocation USD 650 billion
key_developments.description: IMF historic SDR allocation USD 650 billion effective 23 August 2021 — formal IMF COVID-19 + Global recovery SDR allocation framework operational.

key_developments.title: IMF RST established
key_developments.description: IMF Resilience and Sustainability Trust RST effective 1 May 2022 — formal IMF long-term-climate-and-pandemic-resilience financing framework operational.

key_developments.title: IMF 16th General Review of Quotas concluded
key_developments.description: IMF 16th General Review of Quotas concluded 15 December 2023 with 50% equiproportional quota increase + voting-share-redistribution framework — formal IMF 16th Quota Reform framework adopted.

structural_findings: IMF Articles of Agreement operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places IMF Articles of Agreement in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-monetary-cooperation framework + foundational Bretton Woods framework alongside World Bank framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-monetary-cooperation framework + foundational Bretton Woods framework alongside World Bank framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal weighted-voting framework via quota-based-voting framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal SDR Special Drawing Rights framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IMF Conditionality framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Soviet-non-participation framework + Cold War-era unique framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal IMF SDR framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal supplementary international reserve asset framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal currency basket framework with USD + EUR + JPY + GBP + CNY.
Central jurisdiction of formal IMF Conditionality framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Conditionality framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal IMF Quota Reform framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal voting-power redistribution framework with operational Quota Reform framework including BRICS Plus emerging-economies share increase framework.

191 IMF member state ratification framework with Andorra + Cuba + North Korea + Liechtenstein + Monaco + Nauru + Vatican non-members framework is structurally distinctive globally — substantial universal coverage framework.

1944 Bretton Woods + 1945 entry into force + 1947 operations begin + 1969 SDR + 1971 Bretton Woods collapse + 1978 Second Amendment + 2008 Global Financial Crisis + 2016 14th Quota Review + SDR CNY + 2021 SDR allocation + 2022 RST + 2023 16th Quota Review IMF-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

IMF Articles + First Amendment SDR + Second Amendment Floating Exchange Rate + Quota Reform + RST + SDR Currency Basket formal sextet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal IMF-Conditionality-on-Social-Spending framework affecting family-policy-spending framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.
editorial_notes: IMF Articles of Agreement jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global Bretton-Woods foundational-IMF-treaty meta-framework (IMF Articles 1944/1945/1947 + First Amendment SDR 1969 + Second Amendment Floating Exchange Rate 1978 + 14th Quota Review 2016 + SDR CNY 2016 + 2021 SDR allocation USD 650 billion + RST 2022 + 16th Quota Review 2023 + Bretton Woods collapse 1971 + Global Financial Crisis 2008). Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-monetary-cooperation framework globally + foundational Bretton Woods framework alongside World Bank + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal weighted-voting framework via quota-based-voting + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal SDR framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IMF Conditionality framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Soviet-non-participation framework + central jurisdiction of formal SDR Currency Basket USD + EUR + JPY + GBP + CNY + central jurisdiction of formal IMF Conditionality framework + central jurisdiction of formal Quota Reform voting-power redistribution + 191 IMF member states + Andorra + Cuba + North Korea + Liechtenstein + Monaco + Nauru + Vatican non-members + 1944 Bretton Woods + 1945 entry into force + 1947 operations begin + 1969 SDR + 1971 Bretton Woods collapse + 1978 Second Amendment + 2008 Global Financial Crisis + 2016 14th Quota Review + SDR CNY + 2021 SDR allocation + 2022 RST + 2023 16th Quota Review IMF-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: IMF Conditionality framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal IMF-Conditionality-on-Social-Spending framework affecting family-policy-spending framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework relative to other global supranational frameworks.

Joins global + supranational + Bretton-Woods-foundational-IMF-treaty cluster + IMF-Articles-only-global-supranational-binding-treaty-on-international-monetary-cooperation-globally-distinctive + foundational-Bretton-Woods-framework-alongside-World-Bank + IMF-weighted-voting-quota-based + IMF-SDR-1969-Currency-Basket-USD-EUR-JPY-GBP-CNY + IMF-Conditionality + Structural-Adjustment-Programmes-SAPs + Stand-By-Arrangements-SBA + Extended-Fund-Facility-EFF + Poverty-Reduction-and-Growth-Trust-PRGT + Resilience-and-Sustainability-Trust-RST-2022 + IMF-Soviet-non-participation-Cold-War-unique + 191-IMF-member-states + non-members-Andorra-Cuba-North-Korea-Liechtenstein-Monaco-Nauru-Vatican + 14th-Quota-Review-2016-BRICS-Plus + SDR-CNY-Renminbi-2016 + 2021-SDR-allocation-650-billion + 2023-16th-Quota-Review + 1971-Bretton-Woods-collapse-Nixon-Shock + 1978-Second-Amendment-floating-rates + 2008-Global-Financial-Crisis + IMF-Managing-Director-Washington-traditional-European + IMF-Conditionality-on-Social-Spending-PA-intersection clusters within the corpus.
